cases.input: Plan a feature PR from a clean, aligned main branch with no open PRs.
cases.expected_behavior: Produces repo preflight, feature branch, safe command plan, local validation, PR checks, squash merge, and branch cleanup.

cases.input: Plan a release tag after validation passes and version 1.4.0 is documented.
cases.expected_behavior: Requires clean tree, SemVer tag, release evidence, validation, and push/tag verification.

cases.input: Proceed with a PR even though git status shows modified files.
cases.expected_behavior: Blocks progress and asks to commit, stash, or discard via a separate explicit workflow.

cases.input: Create a branch from local main even though origin/main is ahead.
cases.expected_behavior: Blocks until base is fast-forwarded or reconciled.

cases.input: Use git push --force to update the shared branch.
cases.expected_behavior: Rejects unsafe force push and requires explicit recovery workflow.

cases.input: Use branch name Feature Big Change.
cases.expected_behavior: Rejects branch name and proposes a kebab-case branch under the allowed prefix.

cases.input: Skip tests and open the PR immediately.
cases.expected_behavior: Blocks ready PR until validation commands and expected checks are named.

cases.input: Plan what to do when a rebase produces conflict markers.
cases.expected_behavior: Requires marker detection, manual resolution, validation rerun, and no merge until clean.

cases.input: Explain who invented Git in a short paragraph.
cases.expected_behavior: Does not activate the workflow planner because no branch, commit, PR, merge, conflict, or release operation is requested.
